pages.text:
# DEPARTMENT OF LIBRARY AND ARCHIVES § 171.120

**171.070** [2450; 2451] **Books, use and removal of.** (1) Any person may use the books of the library but the State Librarian shall not permit anyone to take the books out of the library, with the following exceptions:
(a) Members of the legislature when the legislature is in session;
(b) Judges and commissioners of the Court of Appeals, the Attorney-General, the Governor, and other state officials.
(c) Attorneys may take the books to the rooms of the Court of Appeals, for use in the argument of cases there pending.
(2) The State Librarian shall keep a record in which he shall charge each book to the person taking it from the library and he shall see that all books are returned at the proper time.

**171.080** [2451; 2453-1] **Recovery for books not returned and damaged property.** (1) Any person who fails to return a book or other property taken from the library by him, or who damages property of the library, shall be liable for double the value of the thing taken or damage to the property.
(2) The State Librarian shall, in the name of the Commonwealth of Kentucky, sue any such person in any court of Franklin county having jurisdiction of the amount involved.

Refusal to return books to public libraries, penalty, KRS 172.990

**171.090** [2450-1] **College libraries, librarian to furnish certain books.** The State Librarian may, when authorized by the trustees of the Legislative and Law Library, furnish without charge to the library of the University of Kentucky and to the libraries of both white and colored state teachers' colleges such public documents, books and manuscripts in the Legislative and Law Library as are not essential to the performance of the duties of that library.

Public books, librarian to distribute, KRS 57.290

**171.100** [2457] **Exchange of Acts and reports.** The State Librarian shall arrange with the proper official of each state of the United States and of the United States and its territories for the exchange of the Acts of the legislatures of each state and of Congress and for the exchange of the published decisions of their courts of intermediate or last resort. When an exchange is agreed upon, the State Librarian shall send to such officer one copy of each of the published decisions of the Court of Appeals of Kentucky and of the Acts of the General Assembly as soon as they are ready for distribution. If the Acts and reports of those states or the United States are not promptly sent to the State Librarian, as fast as issued, he shall write for them. The State Librarian shall keep a record of all books sent and received under this section, which record shall be for the use of the Court of Appeals in directing purchases.

State reports and Acts, number of copies, duties of librarian, KRS 21.270 to 21.290, 21.310, 57.300 to 57.380
Statute Revision Commission to be furnished Reports and Acts, KRS 447.080

**171.110** [2448] **Purchase and maintenance of books.** The librarian may, with the advice and under the direction of the judges of the Court of Appeals, expend in the purchase of law books and literary and miscellaneous works, and the maintenance of books already in the library, such sums as are appropriated for that purpose. The Department of Finance, on receipt of a voucher signed by the judges of the Court of Appeals or any three of them, shall draw its warrant for the sum so expended.

**171.120** [2452] **Expenditures of librarian; annual report.** The librarian shall, annually, make and lay before the judges of the Court of Appeals before the adjournment of the spring term of court, an affidavit showing the amount he has, during the previous year, expended in the purchase of books, maps or charts, and

1377

# DEPARTMENT OF LIBRARY AND ARCHIVES § 171.240

library service or receiving any service from the extension division, shall make such reports and audits as are required to the State Treasurer, the Library Extension Division, and the United States Commissioner of Education. The State Treasurer and Library Extension Division shall make such reports and audits as may be required by the Federal Government and the United States Commissioner of Education in respect to the expenditure of Federal grants and progress of library service.

**171.190** [2438d-5] **Merit system of employment to be used.** All appointments of personnel and the tenure thereof to the extension division and to such libraries, cities, towns, counties and school districts participating in or benefiting by the provisions of the Federal grants for library service shall be based on merit without regard to political consideration.

Division of personnel efficiency, KRS 42.110 to 42.150

**171.200** [2438d-6] **Extension division to improve and integrate library service.** The extension division shall make and administer plans to lessen inequalities of opportunity for library service, for the maintenance of a cooperative and integrated system of library service throughout the state, for suitable cooperative arrangements with school systems, cooperative agricultural extension service and other appropriate agencies, and to make equitable apportionment of any funds received so as to provide library facilities to both the white and colored races.

**171.210** [2438d-7] **Federal laws; division to give notice of acceptance of.** The extension division shall transmit to the United States Commissioner of Education official notice of acceptance of the provisions of Acts of Congress and legislative enactment pertaining to the matters specified herein.

**171.220** [2438d-8] **Contracts with government agencies for establishing local library service.** The extension division may enter into contracts with any local, state or Federal governmental agency or authority for the purpose of enabling the establishment and maintenance of a local public library service. The contracting parties may establish such districts or units of library service as they may mutually agree upon. The library service within such districts shall be carried on according to plans approved by the Director of Library Extension.

**171.230** [4618-130f] **Application of KRS 171.240 to 171.300.** The provisions of KRS 171.240 to 171.300 shall apply to public libraries and all other libraries supported in whole or in part by public funds, except the following: county law libraries, law and legislative libraries, public school libraries, and libraries in cities, counties, or other governmental units having a population of less than three thousand.

**171.240** [4618-130b] **Board for Certification of Librarians; members; terms; meetings.** (1) In the Library Extension Division there shall be a State Board for the Certification of Librarians, composed of the State Librarian, the head of the Library Extension Division, and five members appointed by the Governor from a list submitted by the Board of Directors of the Kentucky Library Association. Two members shall be full-time professional librarians in active public library work, two shall be full-time professional librarians in institutions of higher learning and one shall be a library trustee.

(2) The term of office of each appointive member shall be four years. The first appointive members of the board shall be appointed for terms beginning July 1, 1938; one for a term of one year; one for a term of two years; one for a term of three years; and two for terms of four years. Vacancies shall be filled by appointment for the unexpired terms in the same manner as original appointments are made. The members shall receive no compensation except actual and necessary expenses, incurred in attending meetings, not to exceed ten dollars each, per meeting.
